column4: ROS 2 is a complete redesign of ROS 1 (Robot Operating System) to address limitations in security, real-time performance, and multi-robot support. Uses DDS (Data Distribution Service) for communication instead of a custom middleware. Has annual releases with LTS versions every two years. Latest LTS: Jazzy Jalisco (May 23, 2024, supported until May 2029).

column4: Installation via apt on Ubuntu is straightforward and well-documented. The official documentation at docs.ros.org provides clear, step-by-step instructions. The only non-trivial step is manually adding the ROS 2 apt repository. Requires specific Ubuntu version matching the distribution (e.g., Ubuntu 24.04 for Jazzy).

column4: Highly reusable modular architecture based on packages. Client library interfaces (rcl) available for C++ (rclcpp) and Python (rclpy). Supports composition to combine multiple nodes into a single process. Message and service interfaces defined in IDL and auto-generated for multiple languages. Compatible with ROS 1 via ros1_bridge.
